title: Technical RAG Assistant
summary: Exploring Retrieval-Augmented Generation for technical documentation — what changes when answers must trace back to source spans.
body: A learning project, not a production claim. I'm working through chunking, embeddings, hybrid retrieval, and grounded synthesis on a small corpus of technical specs.
Goal: understand the failure modes well enough to teach them — what breaks when context windows fill, where rerankers help, how faithfulness is actually measured.

title: MCP-Enabled Agent
summary: Working through the Model Context Protocol — typed tool contracts, stateless servers, the architectural patterns behind agentic systems.
body: A small learning study to build the muscle of working with agentic tooling. Bridging old habits from hardware-trust models into how to make AI agents auditable.
Implements a few MCP servers (file, search) and an MCP-aware host. Reading more than writing at this stage.

title: Hardware Trojan Detection Unit for NoC-based MPSoC
summary: A runtime detection mechanism for hardware Trojans embedded in third-party NoC IP. Modified the BookSim simulator with a Trojan model in one router and a Hardware Trojan Detection Unit (HTDU) that authenticates packets before they leave a core.
body: Built on the BookSim simulator and extended in C++ with a configurable Trojan in one router that activates on a rare bit sequence and leaks information.
Designed HTDUs that attach lightweight integrity tags before injection and verify them on egress. Tags are per-flow and sized adaptively to traffic load.
Implemented the hardware version of the HTDU in Verilog to measure area, power, and timing overheads — published with three IEEE papers.

title: Maximising the Lifetime of a Wireless Sensor Network
summary: Python framework that randomly places sensor and target nodes in a field, then uses a meta-heuristic to pick which sensors to activate so total target coverage is maintained while battery life is extended.
body: Implemented the WSN driver with configurable field size, node count, and sensing range distributions.
Built the shuffled ARSH-FATI meta-heuristic and benchmarked against LEACH-family baselines.
Visualised energy depletion and coverage over time for paper figures.

title: Student Contribution Playground
summary: A small C library of utility functions (strings, math, file helpers) used as a sandbox for students to learn real contribution workflow — branch, PR, code review, tests — before they touch their capstone repos.
body: Designed to mimic the conventions of a real open-source project: contribution guide, issue templates, CODEOWNERS, CI on every PR.
Students add new functions, fix planted bugs, and improve docs. Every change goes through the same review cycle a junior engineer would face at work.
Open to forks — the goal is for other educators to remix it for their own programming courses.

title: Single-Cycle + Pipelined Processor with Assembly Interpreter
summary: Three-part educational design: a single-cycle CPU built from scratch in VHDL, a pipelined version with a hazard-detection unit, and a Python interpreter that converts assembly to machine code for the custom ISA.
body: Defined a small ISA and implemented instruction/data memory, ALU, control unit, and register file in VHDL.
Added a 5-stage pipeline with bypass logic and a hazard-detection unit that handles control and data hazards.
Wrote a Python interpreter that lowers a curated assembly subset to machine code; verified end-to-end in ModelSim.

title: USB-Based PIC Microcontroller Development Kit
summary: A PIC microcontroller development board designed for undergraduates studying microcontroller interfacing. Includes ADC/DAC, LEDs, seven-segment displays, serial port, LCD, EEPROM, switches, temperature sensor, buzzer, I2C/SPI, and relay interfaces.
body: Designed the board in Altium Designer; managed the schematic, layout, and BOM.
Wrote teaching example firmware in C demonstrating each peripheral.
Used by subsequent cohorts of microcontroller interfacing students.

title: Library Management System
summary: Web-based library management app for personal/home collections. Admin can add, edit, delete, and shelf-organise books; users browse with filters.
body: Built front-end with HTML, CSS, JavaScript; back-end in PHP with a MySQL store.
Designed admin workflows for shelving and check-out, including a search-with-filters UI for readers.

title: Note App
summary: A small client-side note-taking app — create, edit, delete notes in the browser.
body: Pure HTML/CSS/JavaScript, no build step, persisted to localStorage.
Deployed on Netlify as a quick teaching example for web fundamentals.
